entry.text: The building is recognisable by its scale alone — warehouse dimensions, collapsed roof partially replaced by blue tarp staked to the girders, a sign that once said something optimistic about bulk savings now reading only partial letters. The entrance has dissolved into grey quicksand: fine particulate mixed with groundwater, slowly consuming the threshold. Half-buried in it, still moving, is the centipede — three metres long, armoured in plate segments, feeding with calm efficiency on something that has been in the quicksand long enough not to be a problem anymore. It hears you. Its front section rotates. The eyes are not what you expected.

choices.label: Use ammo to kill it from range
Lure it away from the entrance, then slip past

centipede_dead.text: It takes more than expected — the armour plating is genuine — but it eventually stops. The bulk store interior is a ruin, but a productive one: pallet after pallet of sealed goods, decades old but preserved by the structure's temperature stability. You take as much as you can carry and come back for more.

slipped_inside.text: The interior smells like forty years of sealed goods slowly becoming something else. But the structure held most of the pallets upright and the seals on the containers are remarkable. A bulk haul of pre-war supplies — the kind of thing you tell stories about later.

centipede_reaches_you.text: A segment catches you before you can finish it — armoured mandibles closing on your pack and one shoulder simultaneously. You tear free and pump everything you have left into it at close range. The wound is significant and you're going to feel this for days.

quicksand_caught.text: The quicksand takes your boot and keeps it. You pull free with one sock, landing hard on the dry side. The centipede is already reorienting. You run. You are inside, technically. Also shoeless on one foot. You improvise.
